description_long: This is a weathered wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and narrowing towards the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each showing signs of age, such as dark stains, mossy green patches, and subtle cracks. Two prominent metal hoops, one near the top and one near the bottom, reinforce the structure and display a rusty, oxidized finish. The top surface is flat and made of wood, tightly sealed, suggesting the barrel is closed and possibly used for storage. There are no visible handles or openings, and the overall appearance suggests it has been exposed to the elements for some time. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban alleys, storage yards, or as decorative elements in parks and public spaces.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with rusty metal hoops and mossy stains, featuring a flat, sealed wooden top. The surface shows signs of age and outdoor exposure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the bulging wooden staves, rusty metal hoops, and green moss streaks most prominently.
description_view_1: Side view with a clear look at the vertical wood grain, metal banding, and more pronounced mossy patches.
description_view_2: Another side view highlighting the curvature of the barrel and the sealed wooden top, with less visible moss.
description_view_3: Rear view with darker wood tones, visible metal hoops, and streaks of green moss along the staves.
